title: A Novel ECG - based identification scheme for wearable devices
venue: 2023 6th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Pattern Recognition (AIPR)

method.input_representation: Gramian Angular Summation Field (GASF) images of size 28x28 derived from QRS complexes (250 sample points)
method.training_strategy: Cross entropy loss, Adam optimizer (learning rate 0.001), dropout (p=0.5), 70/30 train-test split

evaluation_protocol: Cross-validation using a 70% training and 30% testing split

reported_limitations: Easy loss of physiological signal events of wearable devices

key_contribution: Proposed a lightweight 2D-CNN using depthwise separable convolutions combined with GASF to process 1D ECG signals for biometric identification
main_strength: Reduced parameter count suitable for resource-constrained wearable devices
main_weakness: Evaluation conducted on a very small subset of the dataset (10 users)
Lack of real-world environmental testing and security/spoofing analysis
insights.notes_for_review: The paper reports 'Accuracy', 'Sensitivity', and 'Specificity', but does not explicitly report the target metrics (AUC, EER, FAR, FRR). While Specificity is related to FAR, the prompt forbids inference.
